criteria.priority_notes: No explicit weight stated in topic source documents. Call-specific technical priorities for Excellence assessment derived from scope text include: advances in AI agent autonomy and self-planning/self-optimization; innovation in memory-augmented agents for long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; development of advanced multi-agent frameworks including mixed AI architectures; multimodal reasoning for real-world applications; robust planning and search mechanisms for complex task decomposition; and integration of external tools and APIs. Proposals must also incorporate structured validation and monitoring techniques.
criteria.assumption_note: Standard Horizon Europe RIA evaluation criterion. The criterion name and its role in evaluation are not stated in the topic text or call extract; they are adopted from standard RIA instrument practice. The criterion weight is not stated in any source document for this topic. Priority notes are derived from explicit scope text describing required and encouraged technical focus areas. Must be verified against Tier 2A evaluation form templates for the RIA instrument.

criteria.priority_notes: No explicit weight stated in topic source documents. Call-specific impact priorities derived from scope text include: contribution to the European Apply AI Strategy; mandatory cohesion activities with ADRA and GenAI4EU central Hub (HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18); deployment via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs); sharing of results via the AI-on-demand platform; linkage to AI Factories and Data Labs resources; and development of synergies with international, European, national, or regional initiatives. Portfolio selection procedure gives priority to proposals targeting healthcare, advanced manufacturing, or in-vehicle autonomous driving sectors.
criteria.assumption_note: Standard Horizon Europe RIA evaluation criterion. The criterion name and its role in evaluation are not stated in the topic text or call extract; they are adopted from standard RIA instrument practice. The criterion weight is not stated in any source document for this topic. Priority notes are derived from explicit scope text requirements on cohesion activities, platform linkage, and Apply AI Strategy contribution. Must be verified against Tier 2A evaluation form templates for the RIA instrument.

criteria.criterion_id: Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation
criteria.criterion_name: Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation
criteria.priority_notes: No explicit weight stated in topic source documents. Call-specific implementation priorities derived from scope text include: mandatory incorporation of qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking mechanisms, and progress monitoring; building on and reusing public results from previously funded relevant actions where possible; collaboration with relevant prior projects; and single-stage submission procedure (deadline-model: single-stage).
criteria.assumption_note: Standard Horizon Europe RIA evaluation criterion. The criterion name and its role in evaluation are not stated in the topic text or call extract; they are adopted from standard RIA instrument practice. The criterion weight is not stated in any source document for this topic. Priority notes on KPI and benchmarking requirements are derived from explicit scope text. Must be verified against Tier 2A evaluation form templates for the RIA instrument.
